text: Yes, mom. Got my keys, my wallet, my phone. I'm all set.

speaker: assistant
text: Just checking. You'd be surprised how many times people forget.

speaker: user
text: Not me. I've got my routine down. Oh, but before we go, I need to water my plants.

speaker: assistant
text: Right now? We'll be back in like two hours.

speaker: user
text: I know, but Gerald the monstera is thriving! Got him at Kukje Flower Market a few months ago, and I really don't want to let him dry out.

speaker: assistant
text: You named your plant Gerald?

speaker: user
text: What? It's a good name. Plus, he's basically part of the family now.

speaker: assistant
text: Okay, that's kind of cute, actually. Go water Gerald. I'll grab the bags from the closet.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. This will just take a second.

speaker: assistant
text: No rush. I'm in no hurry.

speaker: user
text: Alright, Gerald's all set. He was looking a little droopy this morning.

speaker: assistant
text: Plants really do tell you when they need water, don't they? I've got the bags ready whenever you are.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, I've learned that the hard way. I killed like three plants before Gerald came along.

speaker: assistant
text: Three? That's not too bad. I think most people go through a plant graveyard before they figure it out.

speaker: user
text: True. So what's the plan for today? Are we doing this move pretty quick?

speaker: assistant
text: I was thinking we could knock out the bedroom first, then tackle the kitchen. Sound good?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, that works. The bedroom stuff is mostly boxes anyway. Kitchen will take longer with all the dishes.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh man, the dishes. I didn't even think about that. Do you have enough boxes for them?

speaker: user
text: I've got some newspaper too, so I'll wrap everything. Better safe than sorry with the good plates.

speaker: assistant
text: Smart thinking. There's nothing worse than showing up to a new place with broken dishes.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. Oh, I should probably eat something before we start. I haven't had breakfast yet.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, you'll need the energy. What are you thinking? Toast? Cereal?

speaker: user
text: Maybe just some eggs and toast. Quick and filling. You want some?

speaker: assistant
text: I could eat. That sounds perfect, actually. How do you like your eggs?

speaker: user
text: Scrambled, over easy, doesn't really matter. I'm not picky in the morning.

speaker: assistant
text: Same. I'll just grab a couple while you're cooking. Need any help in the kitchen?

speaker: user
text: Nah, I've got it. You could maybe grab the butter and jam from the fridge though?

speaker: assistant
text: On it. This is actually kind of nice, you know? Doing normal stuff before the chaos starts.

speaker: user
text: Right? Once we start moving boxes, it's going to be nonstop. Might as well enjoy the calm.

speaker: assistant
text: How many trips do you think we'll need to make?

speaker: user
text: I'm guessing three, maybe four if the big stuff takes up a lot of space in the truck.

speaker: assistant
text: That's not too bad. We could probably be done by tonight if we keep moving.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, I'm hoping so. I'd like to at least have my bed set up in the new place tonight.

speaker: assistant
text: Totally reasonable. Nothing worse than sleeping on the floor after a long day of moving.

speaker: user
text: Oh man, don't remind me. I did that once and my back was sore for a week.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, that's rough. We'll make sure your bed is priority number one then.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. Okay, eggs are almost ready. Just a couple more minutes.

speaker: assistant
text: Perfect. I'm actually getting hungry now. The smell is making it real.

speaker: user
text: Ha, good. Here, grab a plate. Toast should be done in a second too.

speaker: assistant
text: This looks great. You know, I'm going to miss having you as a neighbor.

speaker: user
text: Aw, come on. You'll still see me. Plus, the new place isn't that far away.

speaker: assistant
text: I know, I know. It's just nice having someone cool in the building, you know?

speaker: user
text: Well, you can come over whenever you want. We can have breakfast again.

speaker: assistant
text: I'm holding you to that. This is really good, by the way. You're a solid cook.

speaker: user
text: Thanks! Nothing fancy, but I can throw together a decent meal. That's what matters.

speaker: assistant
text: Definitely. Okay, I'm going to inhale this and then we can get started on the bedroom?

speaker: user
text: Sounds good. I'll clear these plates real quick while you finish up.

speaker: assistant
text: You don't have to do that. Just leave them, we can worry about dishes later.

speaker: user
text: Nah, I'd rather not have them sitting in the sink all day. Just a quick rinse.

speaker: assistant
text: Fair enough. You're very tidy, I respect that.

speaker: user
text: It's just easier to stay on top of it. Once dishes pile up, it's a nightmare.

speaker: assistant
text: True. I'm more of a 'deal with it later' person, but I get the appeal of staying ahead of it.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, I learned that lesson too. I try to drink 2 liters a day. My mint green Hydroflask goes everywhere with me, so I end up with way more cups to wash if I don't stay on top of it.

speaker: assistant
text: Two liters a day? That's solid hydration. And I like the commitment to the Hydroflask.

speaker: user
text: Right? It keeps everything cold for hours. Plus, it's pretty and I actually remember to drink water because I have it with me.

speaker: assistant
text: That makes sense. I should probably do something like that instead of forgetting to drink water until I'm parched.

speaker: user
text: Honestly, it's a game changer. Makes the whole hydration thing way less of a chore.

speaker: assistant
text: I might have to steal that idea from you. Okay, I'm done eating. Let's tackle this bedroom.

speaker: user
text: Perfect. Let me just grab the first stack of boxes and we can get rolling.

speaker: assistant
text: I'll start loading these into the hallway. This is really happening, huh?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, new chapter. I'm excited and terrified at the same time.

speaker: assistant
text: That's the perfect way to feel about a move. You've got this though.

speaker: user
text: Alright, hand me that tape dispenser. I need to seal up these boxes before we move them.

speaker: assistant
text: Here you go. Man, you've accumulated a lot of stuff in this place.

speaker: user
text: I know, right? I keep finding things I forgot I even owned. Like, why do I have four coffee makers?

speaker: assistant
text: Four? Okay, that's excessive. Are you keeping all of them?

speaker: user
text: No way. I'm donating at least two. The other two actually work, so I might keep those as backups.

speaker: assistant
text: Smart thinking. You never know when you'll need a backup coffee maker. That's basically a life necessity.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. Coffee is non-negotiable for me in the morning. I'm useless without it.

speaker: assistant
text: How many cups do you usually have before you feel human?

speaker: user
text: Usually two, sometimes three if I'm really tired. Depends on how I slept the night before.

speaker: assistant
text: What's your sleep like usually? Are you a good sleeper?

speaker: user
text: Eh, it's hit or miss. Some nights I'm out like a light, other nights I'm tossing and turning until 3 AM.

speaker: assistant
text: That's rough. Do you think it's stress or just your schedule?

speaker: user
text: Probably a mix of both. Work's been intense lately, and I've been staying up too late scrolling on my phone.

speaker: assistant
text: Classic mistake. Have you tried putting it across the room so you can't reach it?

speaker: user
text: I've thought about it, but then I worry I won't hear my alarm. It's a dumb excuse, I know.

speaker: assistant
text: You could use an actual alarm clock. They still exist, believe it or not.

speaker: user
text: You know what? That's not a bad idea. I could grab one at Target when I'm getting stuff for the new place.

speaker: assistant
text: See, problem solved. Now you can banish your phone and sleep better.

speaker: user
text: I'll try it. No promises though. Old habits die hard.

speaker: assistant
text: Fair enough. Okay, this box is getting heavy. Where do you want me to put it?

speaker: user
text: Just stack it by the door with the others. We'll load the truck in batches.

speaker: assistant
text: Got it. How many trips do you think we'll need?

speaker: user
text: Probably three or four, depending on how we pack the truck. I'm hoping to do it all in one day.

speaker: assistant
text: That's ambitious. When's the truck reserved until?

speaker: user
text: Midnight. So we've got plenty of time, but I don't want to be loading boxes at 11 PM.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, that sounds miserable. Let's try to knock it out by dinner time.

speaker: user
text: My thoughts exactly. Oh, and I should probably pack up my bathroom stuff soon. I've been avoiding that.

speaker: assistant
text: Why are you avoiding it? Is it just a lot of stuff?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, and I have like ten half-empty bottles of lotion and shampoo. It's embarrassing.

speaker: assistant
text: Everyone has that. Just throw out the stuff you don't use and pack the rest.

speaker: user
text: You're right. I'm going to do a purge. New place, fresh start and all that.

speaker: assistant
text: That's the spirit. Okay, I'm going to grab another box. You want some water first?

speaker: user
text: Actually, yeah. I'm parched. There should be some bottles in the fridge.

speaker: assistant
text: Perfect. Be right back.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. And hey, seriously, I appreciate you helping with this. It would be a nightmare alone.

speaker: assistant
text: Are you kidding? This is what friends do. Plus, I'm curious about the new place. When can I come over?

speaker: user
text: Once I'm unpacked and the place looks somewhat decent. Give me like a week.

speaker: assistant
text: A week? You're optimistic. I'll give you two weeks and lower my expectations.

speaker: user
text: Fair. Honestly, unpacking might take longer than moving. I have two MacBooks somehow. Personal M3 Air 15-inch, work gives me an M3 Pro. I need to figure out where to set those up.

speaker: assistant
text: Wait, you have two work laptops? That seems redundant.

speaker: user
text: I know, right? The personal one is older, but I still use it for side projects. The work one is for, well, work. I need to set up a proper desk situation.

speaker: assistant
text: That makes sense. Your new place has good desk space?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, actually. There's a little nook in the second bedroom that I'm planning to turn into an office slash creative space.

speaker: assistant
text: Nice. That's ideal. You'll be way more productive with a dedicated workspace.

speaker: user
text: That's the hope. No more working from my couch like I'm doing now.

speaker: assistant
text: Your back probably thanks you for that decision. Couch work is brutal long-term.

speaker: user
text: Tell me about it. I've had the worst neck pain lately. Maybe a proper setup will help.

speaker: assistant
text: It definitely will. Okay, water break's over. Let's get back to it?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, let's do it. We're on a roll now.

speaker: assistant
text: Absolutely. Next stack?

speaker: user
text: Yep. Let's finish the bedroom before we move to the living room.

speaker: assistant
text: Good call. The bedroom's almost done anyway. Just need to organize those boxes by the closet.

speaker: user
text: Right, those have been sitting there for like two weeks. I keep saying I'll deal with them.

speaker: assistant
text: We can knock them out pretty quick. What's even in them?

speaker: user
text: Honestly, I'm not totally sure. Old clothes, I think? Maybe some books. I should probably go through them properly instead of just shoving them in the closet.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, that's the right move. Might find stuff you actually want to keep or donate.

speaker: user
text: True. Okay, let me start opening these up. You want to help sort?

speaker: assistant
text: For sure. Hand me one.

speaker: user
text: Here. This one's heavy. Definitely books.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh wow, yeah. These are old textbooks. You still need any of these?

speaker: user
text: Nah, I'm done with school. Those can go. What about the other box?

speaker: assistant
text: Clothes. A lot of them. Some of these still have tags on.

speaker: user
text: Really? That's kind of embarrassing. I must've forgotten about them.

speaker: assistant
text: Hey, happens to everyone. You want to keep any of these?

speaker: user
text: Let me see... no, no, maybe this one... yeah, I don't think so. Donate pile.

speaker: assistant
text: Nice. You're being ruthless. I like it. That's how you actually declutter.

speaker: user
text: I know. Usually I just move stuff around and convince myself it's organized.

speaker: assistant
text: That's the classic move. So what time do you usually wake up on weekdays?

speaker: user
text: Like 6:30 most days. Gives me time for coffee and to not feel rushed.

speaker: assistant
text: That's pretty early. You a morning person?

speaker: user
text: Not really, but I've gotten used to it. The mornings are actually quieter and less chaotic if you get up early.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, I get that. There's something nice about having that buffer time before everything starts.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. Versus like, rolling out of bed five minutes before I need to leave. That's stressful.

speaker: assistant
text: Okay, last box. What's this one?

speaker: user
text: Oh, that's probably random stuff. Lamps, cables, things I wasn't sure where to put.

speaker: assistant
text: Cable graveyard. Yeah, that's relatable. Do any of these actually work?

speaker: user
text: I have no idea. I should probably test them. But honestly, I could just throw most of them out.

speaker: assistant
text: Fair. You can always find a charger when you need one these days anyway.

speaker: user
text: True. Alright, I think the bedroom's looking pretty good now. Should we tackle the living room?

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, let's do it. That's the bigger project though.

speaker: user
text: I know. But if we keep the momentum going, we can at least make a dent.

speaker: assistant
text: You're right. Come on, let's move some of this stuff out there.

speaker: user
text: Wait, should we grab some snacks first? I'm getting hungry.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh yeah, good idea. What do you have?

speaker: user
text: Probably some crackers, cheese, maybe some fruit. Nothing fancy.

speaker: assistant
text: That sounds perfect. Quick energy boost.

speaker: user
text: Right. I'll throw something together. You want anything specific?

speaker: assistant
text: Whatever you're having is cool. I'm not picky.

speaker: user
text: Cool. Give me like five minutes. I'll meet you in the living room.

speaker: assistant
text: No problem. I'll start taking inventory of what's in there.

speaker: user
text: Good plan. Yeah, that room is kind of a mess. There's stuff everywhere.

speaker: assistant
text: We'll get it sorted. One section at a time.

speaker: user
text: Okay, I'm back. Got the snacks set up on the coffee table.

speaker: assistant
text: Nice. Alright, so the living room. What's the main issue here?

speaker: user
text: Everything's kind of... scattered. No real organization. And I've got too much furniture for the space, I think.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, I see what you mean. That bookshelf over there is kind of awkwardly placed.

speaker: user
text: I know. I moved it there temporarily like three months ago and just never moved it back.

speaker: assistant
text: So the temporary setup became permanent. Classic.

speaker: user
text: Basically. Okay, let me think about this. What if we move that to the corner instead?

speaker: assistant
text: That could work. It'd open up the middle of the room more.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. And then the couch would have more breathing room.

speaker: assistant
text: Let's try it. On three?

speaker: user
text: One, two, three. Go.

speaker: assistant
text: Okay, pushing... yeah, it's moving. Watch the corner there.

speaker: user
text: Got it. A little more to the left? There, stop. That's perfect.

speaker: assistant
text: Nice. Honestly, that does look better. Way more open now.

speaker: user
text: Right? I can't believe we didn't do this sooner. The apartment feels bigger already.

speaker: assistant
text: Definitely. You want to move the coffee table next, or take a break?

speaker: user
text: Let's take a break. I'm actually starving. When did we start doing this?

speaker: assistant
text: Like two hours ago? Right after you got back from the store.

speaker: user
text: Two hours? Wow. I think I skipped breakfast this morning too. No wonder I'm dying.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, you should probably eat something. I could grab some coffee while you figure out lunch.

speaker: user
text: That sounds amazing. There's stuff in the fridge from yesterday. Maybe I'll make a sandwich or something quick.

speaker: assistant
text: Works for me. Black coffee, right?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, thanks. I'll be in the kitchen.

speaker: assistant
text: Okay, I'll be right back.

speaker: user
text: Hey, while you're making that, can you grab the milk? I want to add a little to mine.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh, sure. I thought you said black?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, I usually do, but I'm feeling a little different today. Just a splash.

speaker: assistant
text: Got it. One splash of milk coming up.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. Oh, and do we have any bread left? I can't remember.

speaker: assistant
text: I think there's a loaf on the counter. Pretty sure it's still good.

speaker: user
text: Okay, cool. I'm going to make a turkey and cheese sandwich. Want one?

speaker: assistant
text: Sure, why not. Turkey and cheese sounds good right now.

speaker: user
text: Perfect. I'll make two. This is going to hit the spot.

speaker: assistant
text: Agreed. You want mustard or mayo?

speaker: user
text: Both, actually. I'm feeling fancy today.

speaker: assistant
text: Ha, I like it. Living on the edge with both condiments.

speaker: user
text: Right? It's the little things. Okay, I'm putting the bread in the toaster now.

speaker: assistant
text: Good call. Toasted bread is always better anyway.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. So while we eat, I was thinking about the bedroom too. Should we rearrange in there?

speaker: assistant
text: Maybe. The bed's pretty heavy though. You thinking about moving it?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, probably not today. But maybe we flip the desk? It gets weird light from the window right now.

speaker: assistant
text: That's actually a good idea. The afternoon sun is pretty rough in there.

speaker: user
text: Right? Okay, the toast is popping. Sandwiches are almost ready.

speaker: assistant
text: Perfect timing. I'll grab some plates and napkins.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. Oh, and can you see if there are any chips in the pantry? I'm craving something salty.

speaker: assistant
text: Let me check. Yeah, there's a bag of barbecue chips in here.

speaker: user
text: Oh perfect. Grab those. This is going to be a solid lunch.

speaker: assistant
text: Agreed. Here, I'm putting everything on the table.

speaker: user
text: Great. Okay, sandwiches are ready. Come on, let's sit down.

speaker: assistant
text: Yes, finally. I'm actually pretty hungry too.

speaker: user
text: This looks good. Thanks for helping with the furniture thing, by the way.

speaker: assistant
text: No problem. I was getting tired of the layout anyway. This is better.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, way better. You know what else I need to do? Actually listen to music more. I've been forgetting about it.

speaker: assistant
text: Music's always nice. You have good speakers set up anywhere?

speaker: user
text: Not really, just my headphones. AirPods Pro for the subway, Sony XM5s for when I need to focus at home or the office. But I should get actual speakers for the living room.

speaker: assistant
text: That would be cool. Good speakers really make a difference with music.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. Maybe I'll look into it after lunch. Nothing too expensive, just something decent.

speaker: assistant
text: Smart. You don't need anything crazy for an apartment anyway.

speaker: user
text: Right. Okay, this sandwich is really hitting the spot. I'm so glad we did this furniture thing.

speaker: assistant
text: Same. And now the apartment actually feels different, you know? In a good way.

speaker: user
text: For sure. Maybe we tackle the coffee table after we eat?

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, let's finish up here first. Then we can see how it all comes together.

speaker: user
text: Sounds good. Want me to grab you anything else while I'm up?

speaker: assistant
text: Oh, I'm good for now. Maybe some water though?

speaker: user
text: Coming right up.

speaker: assistant
text: Thanks. So what time were you thinking of hitting the store for the coffee table?

speaker: user
text: I was thinking maybe around 3? Gives us time to digest and clean up a bit.

speaker: assistant
text: That works. I have nothing going on this afternoon.

speaker: user
text: Perfect. Oh, and I need to do laundry soon. My work clothes are piling up.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, I noticed you've been wearing the same rotation lately.

speaker: user
text: Ha, busted. I've just been lazy about it.

speaker: assistant
text: Hey, no judgment. Laundry is the worst.

speaker: user
text: Right? And then folding it all... ugh.

speaker: assistant
text: Do you at least have a system or do you just wing it?

speaker: user
text: I hang most stuff up straight from the dryer. Saves me folding time.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh, that's smart. Less wrinkles too, probably.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. I learned that the hard way after showing up somewhere looking all wrinkled.

speaker: assistant
text: We've all been there. So what's your wake-up routine like on work days?

speaker: user
text: Pretty simple. Alarm at 6:30, snooze it once, then I'm up by 6:45.

speaker: assistant
text: That's pretty disciplined. I usually need like three snoozes minimum.

speaker: user
text: I used to be like that. But I realized I was just making myself more tired.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, that makes sense. One snooze is better than five.

speaker: user
text: Right. Then I shower, get dressed, grab coffee. Out the door by 7:15.

speaker: assistant
text: That's a solid routine. You must be one of those morning people.

speaker: user
text: Not really. I'm just more functional if I don't rush.

speaker: assistant
text: Fair enough. Do you eat breakfast before you leave?

speaker: user
text: Usually just coffee and maybe a banana. I eat a real breakfast at work.

speaker: assistant
text: Smart. Gives you something to look forward to.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. Plus I'm not really hungry that early anyway.

speaker: assistant
text: I'm the opposite. I'm starving the second I wake up.

speaker: user
text: You should try having something light the night before. Might help.

speaker: assistant
text: Maybe. I usually just crash hard and wake up ravenous.

speaker: user
text: How many hours are you sleeping usually?

speaker: assistant
text: Probably like 6 or 7. Could be more though.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, that's on the lower end. I try to get 8.

speaker: assistant
text: Good for you. I'm bad at getting to bed early enough.

speaker: user
text: What time do you usually head to bed?

speaker: assistant
text: Like 11 or 11:30 if I'm being good. Often midnight though.

speaker: user
text: And you're up when?

speaker: assistant
text: Around 7. So yeah, definitely not enough sleep most nights.

speaker: user
text: You should try to shift that earlier. You'd probably feel better.

speaker: assistant
text: I know, I know. I'm just addicted to my phone before bed.

speaker: user
text: Oh man, same. That's the killer.

speaker: assistant
text: Right? Like I know I should put it down but then it's 11:30 and I'm still scrolling.

speaker: user
text: I've been trying to do no phones an hour before bed. It actually helps.

speaker: assistant
text: See, I know that works but I can never stick to it.

speaker: user
text: It takes like two weeks to form the habit.

speaker: assistant
text: Maybe I'll try it. Speaking of routines, how's your heating situation in here?

speaker: user
text: Actually really good. I love ondol heating. Keep it at 22 degrees, my electricity bill is actually reasonable.

speaker: assistant
text: Oh nice. That's a good temperature. Not too hot, not too cold.

speaker: user
text: Exactly. And it heats evenly throughout the apartment. Way better than the old place.

speaker: assistant
text: That's great. Okay, so we should probably wrap up and start thinking about that coffee table run.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, let me just clean up these dishes real quick.

speaker: assistant
text: Sure, I'll grab my keys while you do that.

speaker: user
text: Thanks. Should only take like five minutes. These pans are still warm at least.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, that makes it easier. Hot water helps.

speaker: user
text: Definitely. Nothing worse than dried-on food.

speaker: assistant
text: So true. I hate scrubbing forever.

speaker: user
text: Okay, almost done. Just need to wipe down the sink.

speaker: assistant
text: Take your time. No rush.

speaker: user
text: Done! Let me just grab my wallet and we can head out.

speaker: assistant
text: Perfect. Do you know what style you're looking for?

speaker: user
text: Honestly, just something simple and not too expensive. My current one is falling apart.

speaker: assistant
text: Fair enough. The furniture place usually has some decent options.

speaker: user
text: Yeah, I've heard good things. Plus they're not far from here.

speaker: assistant
text: Right, like fifteen minutes by car?

speaker: user
text: Yeah, something like that. Should we take your car or mine?

speaker: assistant
text: Mine's closer to the door. Let's just use that.

speaker: user
text: Cool. Oh wait, let me turn off the stove. I left the burner on low.

speaker: assistant
text: Good call. Safety first.

speaker: user
text: Okay, it's off now. Ready to go.

speaker: assistant
text: Great. Let me just lock up the apartment.

speaker: user
text: Actually, I'll do it. You start the car and get it warmed up.

speaker: assistant
text: Sounds good. I'll meet you down there.

speaker: user
text: Be there in like two minutes.

speaker: assistant
text: No problem. I'll have it running.

speaker: user
text: Alright, heading down now. Did you grab your phone?

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, it's in my pocket. All set.

speaker: user
text: Perfect. See you in the parking lot.

speaker: assistant
text: See you in a sec.

speaker: user
text: Okay, I'm here. Car's nice and warm already.

speaker: assistant
text: Right? The heat works great in this thing.

speaker: user
text: Way better than my old car. That one took forever to warm up.

speaker: assistant
text: Yeah, older cars are rough. This one's pretty reliable.

speaker: user
text: So how's your week been besides all this?

speaker: assistant
text: Pretty busy, honestly. Work's been kind of insane.

speaker: user
text: Yeah? What's going on?

speaker: assistant
text: Just a bunch of meetings and deadlines piling up. Nothing too unusual.

speaker: user
text: That sounds stressful. At least the weekend's coming up.

speaker: assistant
